structure.content: A Knowledge Object is the smallest canonical semantic unit recognized by a Canonical Knowledge Structure.
Canonical identity is an immutable, globally unique identifier that distinguishes one Knowledge Object from another.
Every Canonical Knowledge Structure shall consist exclusively of Knowledge Objects.
Two admissible structural projections of the same Knowledge Structure preserve identical canonical semantics.
By definition, both projections originate from the same Knowledge Structure. By Canonical Law 2, admissible projections preserve canonical semantics.
